item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.5 No.6, 1957
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE No.6(90) Vol. V 1957
…solitary griffin each day; and darely[?] for new harmony… Sir Philip Sydney (1590)
pages.handwritten_text:
PARIS Les collections de Haute Couture, Printemps-Ete 1957
Christian Dior opening 1957
sketch_descriptions: A charcoal or pencil sketch of two figures closely engaged, one appearing to be a man with balding hair and the other wearing an elaborate, wide-brimmed hat, possibly a woman in haute couture fashion.
The figures are drawn with expressive, loose lines suggesting a lively scene perhaps at a fashion event or opening.

events: Les collections de Haute Couture Printemps-Ete 1957

pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle captures a dynamic sketch related to the 1957 Paris Haute Couture Spring-Summer collections, highlighting the Christian Dior opening that year and referencing Sir Philip Sydney.

pages.handwritten_text:
PARIS, les collections de haute couture Printemps-Ete 1957
Fabius Mannebergen Nina Ricci
Molyneux Christian Dior
Chanel Muriel La ligne de briefing
Paris Jefferey Horn The Battle Silhouette Spring Collection 1957
Jean Dessès Printemps-Eté 1957 La Perle du Vear
Pierre Balmain
Pierre Cardin
Maggy Rouff
sketch_descriptions: A group of seated figures and a standing fashion model sketch labeled with names Fabius Mannebergen and Nina Ricci.
A crowded indoor scene with people mingling, including artists named Molyneux, Christian Dior, Chanel, and Muriel, annotated 'La ligne de briefing'.
A tall elegant woman in a large blue hat among other figures, labeled Pierre Balmain.
A fashion show or social gathering scene with multiple figures in elaborate hats and gowns, annotated 'Jean Dessès Printemps-Eté 1957 La Perle du Vear'.
A side view of standing models and seated figures observing, labeled Paris, Jefferey Horn, The Battle Silhouette Spring Collection 1957.
A large detailed crowd scene showing audience members facing a runway or stage, with figures in formal and haute couture attire including a woman in a large hat, annotated Pierre Cardin.
A lineup of fashion models wearing large hats and full dresses, labeled Maggy Rouff.

events: les collections de haute couture Printemps-Ete 1957

pages.page_summary: This page captures expressive sketches and handwritten notes of Paris haute couture fashion collections from Spring-Summer 1957, depicting designers, models, and social scenes from high-end fashion events.

pages.typeset_text:
The drawings in this issue were made possible by the invitation of American Harper's Bazaar to attend the opening of the Paris Spring Collections
"TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE" IS PUBLISHED 24 TIMES A YEAR AT 3s. 6d. (50c) A COPY.
YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES (WITH FOLDER AND INDEX) TO ANY-PART OF THE WORLD ARE £4. 4s. ($12 or equivalent); SHORTER PERIODS PRO RATA. AIR MAIL BY QUOTATION.
SUBSCRIPTIONS FROM OUTSIDE THE STERLING AREA MAY BE PAID THROUGH THE UNESCO COUPON SCHEME
Handprinted in Great Britain by Poets'& Partners' Press from blocks made by Auster Miles Ltd and published by Topolski's Chronicles Ltd. from Bridge Arch 138, Sutton Walk, London, S.E.1. Copyright reserved.
865
sketch_descriptions: A stylized sketch of a woman with a dark curly hairstyle seated, holding a cigarette or similar slender object, her figure somewhat abstract with textured shading and line work.
Two dog-like animal figures near the woman; one on the left side with exaggerated features and open eye, the other on the right side looking sideways.
Decorative and abstract background shapes and lines suggesting urban or interior setting with loose, expressive lines.
pages.page_summary: This page features a 1957 sketch by Felix Topolski in Paris showing a woman with two dogs, accompanied by a note crediting American Harper's Bazaar for access to the Paris Spring Collections opening event.
